hypothesis: Augmenting SAM-EM training with an auxiliary temporal-consistency loss that penalizes deviations from physics-informed motion priors (e.g., LEONARDO-predicted frame-to-frame displacement distributions) will reduce particle identity-switching rates by at least 50% on high-density (at least 8 particles) LPTEM videos with liquid thickness ≥100 nm compared to the current SAM-EM trained without such a loss.
